Zilkha joined J.P. Morgan in 1973 and worked in positions of increasing responsibility in tax incentivized transactions, restructuring and mergers & acquisitions. In 1982, he joined James D. Wolfensohn & Company, and in 1987 formed his own investment advisory boutique, Zilkha & Company. By 1994, he acquired 95% of Colt's Manufacturing Company, the historic American firearms manufacturer, restructuring it over 19 years. He subsequently established Zilkha Venture Partners (1999) and Zilkha Partners L.P. (2008), becoming a trustee emeritus of Wesleyan University and member of The Council on Foreign Relations.
achievements: Founded Zilkha & Company, Zilkha Venture Partners, and Zilkha Partners
Successfully acquired and restructured Colt's Manufacturing Company
Became trustee emeritus of Wesleyan University

figures.name: Rouben Zachary Mamoulian
figures.title: Film and Theater Director
figures.roleDescription: Pioneering Armenian-American director of film and Broadway
figures.significance: Revolutionary director who directed acclaimed Broadway musicals Oklahoma! and Carousel, and pioneered innovative cinematic techniques in early sound films.
figures.biography:
Rouben Zachary Mamoulian was born on October 8, 1897, in Tiflis, Russian Empire (now Tbilisi, Georgia), to a family of Armenian descent. His mother, Virginie (née Kalantarian), was from a wealthy family of landowners and financiers and served as a director of Armenian theatre, while his father, Zachary, was a bank president. The family fled Russia during the 1917 revolution due to their Czarist sympathies.

Mamoulian's career spanned both Broadway and Hollywood, where he became known for his innovative directorial techniques. He directed the acclaimed original stagings of Oklahoma! (1943) and Carousel (1945), as well as the first production of George Gershwin's Porgy and Bess (1935). In film, he directed sixteen movies including Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde (1931), Love Me Tonight (1932), Queen Christina (1934), and the first three-strip Technicolor film Becky Sharp (1935). His innovations included restoring mobility to the camera and developing signature uses of montage, close-ups, split-screens and dissolves.

Mamoulian died on December 4, 1987, at age 90 in Woodland Hills, California. He was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame in 1960 and received a Lifetime Achievement Award from the Directors Guild of America in 1982. The Rouben Mamoulian Award for Best Director continues to be presented annually at the Sydney Film Festival.
achievements: Directed original Broadway productions of Oklahoma! and Carousel
Directed first three-strip Technicolor feature film Becky Sharp
Pioneered innovative camera techniques in early sound cinema
Received star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
Received Directors Guild of America Lifetime Achievement Award

figures.name: Virginie Kalantarian Mamoulian
figures.title: Theater Director
figures.roleDescription: Armenian theatre director and matriarch
figures.significance: Director of Armenian theatre and mother of acclaimed director Rouben Mamoulian, from a wealthy family of landowners and financiers.
figures.biography:
Virginie Kalantarian Mamoulian was from a family of wealthy landowners and financiers in the Armenian community of Tiflis, Russian Empire. She served as a director of the Armenian theatre, demonstrating the family's deep involvement in cultural and artistic pursuits. She married Zachary Mamoulian, a bank president, and together they raised their son Rouben and daughter Svetlana in the Armenian Apostolic faith.

The family was sympathetic to the Czarist regime and fled Russia during the turmoil of the 1917 revolution and ensuing civil war. Earlier, in the aftermath of the Russian Revolution of 1905, when ethnic violence arose in Tiflis, the family had moved to Paris for three years. Virginie's influence on her son's artistic development was significant, as the family maintained strong cultural connections and multilingual capabilities throughout their various relocations.

figures.name: Zachary Mamoulian
figures.title: Bank President
figures.roleDescription: Armenian banker and patriarch
figures.significance: Bank president and father of acclaimed director Rouben Mamoulian, who fled Russia during the 1917 revolution due to Czarist sympathies.
figures.biography:
Zachary Mamoulian was a prominent Armenian bank president in Tiflis, Russian Empire (now Tbilisi, Georgia). He married Virginie Kalantarian, who came from a wealthy family of landowners and financiers and served as a director of Armenian theatre. Together they raised their children Rouben and Svetlana in the Armenian Apostolic faith, creating a multilingual household where Russian, Armenian, and Georgian were spoken.

The family was forced to relocate multiple times due to political upheaval. After the Russian Revolution of 1905 and subsequent ethnic violence in Tiflis, they moved to Paris for three years. Later, the Mamoulian family, being sympathetic to the Czarist regime, fled Russia during the turmoil of the 1917 revolution and the ensuing civil war. Zachary's position and the family's wealth allowed them to maintain their cultural connections and provide excellent education for their children despite these disruptions.

figures.name: Svetlana Mamoulian
figures.title: Family Member
figures.roleDescription: Younger sister of director Rouben Mamoulian
figures.significance: Younger sister of acclaimed director Rouben Mamoulian, raised in an Armenian family that fled Russia during the 1917 revolution.
figures.biography:
Svetlana Mamoulian was the younger sister of director Rouben Mamoulian, born to Zachary and Virginie Kalantarian Mamoulian in Tiflis, Russian Empire. She was raised in the Armenian Apostolic faith alongside her brother in a multilingual household where Russian, Armenian, and Georgian were spoken fluently.

Like her brother, Svetlana experienced the family's multiple relocations due to political upheaval in Russia. The family moved to Paris for three years following the Russian Revolution of 1905 and ethnic violence in Tiflis, and later fled Russia entirely during the 1917 revolution due to their Czarist sympathies. Her upbringing in this culturally rich but politically turbulent environment shaped the family's later emphasis on artistic and cultural pursuits.

figures.name: Ezra Zilkha
figures.title: Financier and Philanthropist
figures.roleDescription: Iraqi-American banker and philanthropist
figures.significance: Father of Donald Zilkha and member of prominent Zilkha banking family that established banking operations in the Middle East.
figures.biography:
Ezra Zilkha was the father of Donald Elias Zilkha and a member of the prominent Zilkha banking family that originated from the Middle East. The Zilkha family were bankers from the Middle East, with patriarch Khedouri Zilkha establishing the bank in 1899. The family's banking operations were nationalized in three countries - Iraq, Syria and Egypt - between 1950 to 1956, forcing the family to rebuild their financial empire in the United States and Europe.

Ezra Zilkha played a role in the family's transition to Western operations after the Middle Eastern nationalizations. His son Donald would go on to establish successful investment firms in the United States, continuing the family's financial legacy in their new base of operations.

figures.name: Donald Elias Zilkha
figures.title: Financier and Businessman
figures.roleDescription: American financier and founder of multiple investment firms
figures.significance: Founded Zilkha & Company (1987), Zilkha Venture Partners (1999), and Zilkha Partners (2008), and acquired Colt's Manufacturing Company.
figures.biography:
Donald Elias Zilkha was born on April 8, 1951, in New York to Ezra Zilkha. He is the son of a prominent Middle Eastern banking family - the Zilkha family were bankers whose patriarch Khedouri Zilkha established their bank in 1899. The family faced nationalization in Iraq, Syria and Egypt between 1950-1956, forcing them to rebuild in the US and Europe. Donald attended Lycee Francais de New York before high school at The Hill School, then graduated from Wesleyan University's College of Social Studies in 1973.

Zilkha began his career at J.P. Morgan in 1973, working in tax incentivized transactions, restructuring and mergers & acquisitions. In 1982, he joined James D. Wolfensohn & Company focusing on M&A and venture capital. In 1987, he formed Zilkha & Company, his own investment advisory boutique for blue chip Australian and European companies. By 1994, he acquired 95% of Colt's Manufacturing Company, the historic firearms manufacturer that had struggled in Chapter 11 for four years, restructuring it over 19 years.

In 1999, Zilkha established Zilkha Venture Partners and in 2001 became associated with Yale's Sachem Ventures. He formed Zilkha Partners L.P. in 2008, concentrating on US publicly traded small capitalization companies. Donald is married to Ingrid Virginia Urrutia Soler with three children, and serves as trustee emeritus of Wesleyan University and member of The Council on Foreign Relations.
achievements: Founded Zilkha & Company (1987)
Founded Zilkha Venture Partners (1999)
Founded Zilkha Partners (2008)
Acquired and restructured Colt's Manufacturing Company
Trustee emeritus of Wesleyan University

figures.name: Nathaniel Zilkha
figures.title: Businessman and Music Industry Executive
figures.roleDescription: Co-founder and executive chairman of Firebird Music Holdings and chairman of Gibson
figures.significance: Prominent music industry investor who led Gibson's recovery from bankruptcy and co-founded Firebird Music Holdings with access to $1bn in capital.
figures.biography:
Nathaniel 'Nat' Zilkha was born in 1975 in New York and raised in Portland, Maine. He graduated from Princeton University in 1999 with an AB in politics. A guitarist since grade school, in 1998 he co-founded the band Red Rooster with Princeton classmate Jay Erickson. The folk group evolved into an eight-piece jam band that released three albums and performed at venues including the Newport Folk Festival in 2008.

Zilkha began his finance career at Goldman Sachs, becoming vice president in 2006 before joining KKR in 2007. At KKR, he was appointed head of asset management and global special situations in 2010, became a partner and head of KKR Credit in 2013. In 2018, when KKR acquired majority stake in the bankrupt Gibson Brands, Zilkha served as chairman overseeing the company's return to solvency. As a guitarist, he felt 'bringing Gibson back to what it was supposed to be was almost a responsibility.'

In 2021, Zilkha left KKR to co-found Firebird Music Holdings with former Ticketmaster CEO Nathan Hubbard. With $1bn in capital access, Firebird has invested in Red Light Management, Defected Records, and created partnerships with the D'Amelio family. He also led a $50 million funding round for TVG Hospitality. Zilkha serves on boards including Save the Music Foundation and Apollo Theater.
achievements: Co-founder and executive chairman of Firebird Music Holdings
Chairman of Gibson Brands
Former KKR partner and head of KKR Credit
Led Gibson's recovery from bankruptcy

figures.name: Selim Zilkha
figures.title: Entrepreneur and Founder of Mothercare
figures.roleDescription: Iraqi Jewish entrepreneur who founded Mothercare retail chain
figures.significance: Founded Mothercare, one of the United Kingdom's largest retail chains, and co-owns Zilkha Biomass Energy.
figures.biography:
Selim Zilkha was born in 1927 in Baghdad to an Iraqi Jewish family. He became a prominent entrepreneur who founded Mothercare, which grew to become one of the United Kingdom's largest retail chains specializing in products for mothers and children. His success with Mothercare established him as a significant figure in British retail.

Currently, Zilkha is the co-owner of Zilkha Biomass Energy, which owns timberland and manufactures pellet biofuel in Texas, showing his continued involvement in business ventures focused on sustainable energy. His career represents the successful transition of Iraqi Jewish business families to Western markets following the upheavals in the Middle East during the mid-20th century.
achievements: Founded Mothercare retail chain
Co-owner of Zilkha Biomass Energy

figures.name: Gabrielle Zilkha
figures.title: Filmmaker
figures.roleDescription: Toronto-based filmmaker and documentary maker
figures.significance: Documentary filmmaker who researched the House of Israel community in Ghana for a film project.
figures.biography:
Gabrielle Zilkha is a Toronto-based filmmaker who has worked on documentary projects exploring Jewish communities. In 2012, she visited Sefwe Wiawso in Ghana to conduct research for a documentary about the House of Israel community that she was producing. According to her research, about 200 people—mostly children—live in the House of Israel community in southwestern Ghana.

Zilkha noted that the lack of a historical record makes it difficult to verify the community's claims to Jewish ancestry, though there is an oral tradition dating back 200 years among the Sefwi tribe members who built a synagogue in 1998 and claim roots in the Ten Lost Tribes of ancient Israel.
achievements: Documentary filmmaker researching Jewish communities

figures.name: Khedouri Zilkha
figures.title: Banking Patriarch
figures.roleDescription: Founder of the Zilkha banking empire
figures.significance: Established the Zilkha bank in 1899, founding the family's Middle Eastern banking dynasty.
figures.biography:
Khedouri Zilkha was the patriarch of the Zilkha family and established the family bank in 1899, creating what would become a significant banking presence in the Middle East. His banking establishment formed the foundation of the Zilkha family's financial empire that operated across multiple countries in the region.

The bank founded by Khedouri Zilkha operated successfully until the family faced nationalization across three countries - Iraq, Syria and Egypt - between 1950 to 1956. This forced the Zilkha family to rebuild their financial operations in the United States and Europe, with subsequent generations like his descendant Donald Zilkha establishing new investment firms in America.
achievements: Established the Zilkha bank in 1899
Founded the Zilkha banking dynasty

figures.name: Ahmad Al Ghurair
figures.title: Founder of Al Ghurair Group
figures.roleDescription: Pearl diver turned business founder and patriarch
figures.significance: Founded the Al Ghurair Group in 1960 and established the family business empire after starting as a pearl diver in Dubai in the 1930s.
figures.biography:
Ahmad Al Ghurair began his career as a pearl diver in Dubai during the 1930s alongside his son Saif. The pearl diving business proved successful and grew over the decades. In 1960, as the business expanded significantly, Ahmad Al Ghurair formally established the Al Ghurair Group to oversee and manage the family's diverse business interests.

Ahmad later turned over the management of the family business to his five sons: Saif, Abdulla, Majid, Marwan, and Jomaa, establishing a legacy that would make the Al Ghurair family one of the most prominent merchant families in the UAE. Under the umbrella of the Al Ghurair Group, the family built the UAE's first cement factory, flour mill, and sugar refinery, positioning themselves at the forefront of the country's industrial development.
achievements: Founded Al Ghurair Group in 1960
Built UAE's first cement factory
Built UAE's first flour mill
Built UAE's first sugar refinery

figures.name: Saif Ahmad Al Ghurair
figures.title: Chairman of Al Ghurair Group
figures.roleDescription: Billionaire businessman and chairman of Saif Al Ghurair Group
figures.significance: Led the Al Ghurair Group as chairman and was a fellow billionaire alongside his brother Abdulla, helping establish the family as one of the UAE's most prominent business dynasties.
figures.biography:
Saif Ahmad Al Ghurair (1924-2019) was born into the Al Ghurair merchant family and began his career alongside his father Ahmad as a pearl diver in Dubai during the 1930s. After his father established the Al Ghurair Group in 1960, Saif became chairman of the company, a position he held until the 1990s. Under his leadership, the group expanded into multiple sectors and became one of the largest diversified family business groups in the Middle East.

In the 1990s, Saif and his brother Abdulla decided to split the Al Ghurair Group into two complementary companies. Saif retained the chairmanship of what became known as the Saif Al Ghurair Group, while Abdulla established Al Ghurair Investment. The restructuring was completed by 1994. Saif's group focuses on manufacturing, real estate, and investments, with major projects including the BurJuman Centre and various industrial ventures. He was recognized as a billionaire businessman and remained active in the family business until his death in 2019.
achievements: Chairman of Al Ghurair Group until 1990s
Co-founded Bank of Oman (later Mashreq Bank) in 1967
Opened Gulf's first shopping mall, Al Ghurair Centre, in 1981
Founded Gulf Extrusions in 1976

figures.name: Abdulla bin Ahmad Al Ghurair
figures.title: Founder and Chairman of Mashreq Bank
figures.roleDescription: Billionaire businessman and founder of Mashreq Bank
figures.significance: Founded Mashreq Bank in 1967 and established the Abdulla Al Ghurair Foundation for Education with a $1.14 billion endowment, making him one of the UAE's most prominent businessmen and philanthropists.
figures.biography:
Abdulla bin Ahmad Al Ghurair was born around 1930 and is a member of the prominent Emirati Al Ghurair business family. In 1967, he founded Mashreq Bank in Dubai, which became the largest private bank in the United Arab Emirates by the 1980s. The bank changed its name from Bank of Oman to Mashreq Bank in 1994. In October 2019, he stepped down as Chairman but remained as a board member, having built the institution into a major regional financial player.

Beyond banking, Al Ghurair built a diversified business empire including construction, food, and other ventures. His construction company was involved in building the Dubai Metro and the exterior cladding of the Burj Khalifa, the world's tallest building. Under the Jenan brand, his Al Ghurair Food company sells pasta and other food products. In 1975, he launched Oman Insurance, which rebranded to Sukoon in 2022. He has been recognized as one of the richest individuals in the UAE, with Forbes estimating his net worth at $4.8 billion in 2025. In 2015, he established the Abdulla Al Ghurair Foundation for Education and was appointed as the first Goodwill Ambassador for education in the Arab world by ALECSO in 2016.
achievements: Founded Mashreq Bank in 1967
Built Dubai Metro through construction company
Constructed exterior cladding of Burj Khalifa
Established Abdulla Al Ghurair Foundation in 2015
First Goodwill Ambassador for education in Arab world (2016)

figures.name: Abdul Aziz Al Ghurair
figures.title: Chairman of Mashreq Bank and Former Speaker of Federal National Council
figures.roleDescription: Politician and billionaire businessman
figures.significance: Served as Speaker of the UAE's Federal National Council from 2007-2011 and is Chairman of Mashreq Bank, combining prominent roles in both politics and business.
figures.biography:
Abdul Aziz Al Ghurair was born on July 1, 1954, and is the son of Abdulla Al Ghurair, founder of Mashreq Bank. He trained as an industrial engineer and earned a bachelor's degree from California Polytechnic State University. Joining Mashreq in 1977, he worked in various capacities until 1988, when he took over responsibility for the bank's international operations, establishing branches in New York, London, Bahrain, Qatar, Egypt, India and Pakistan. He was appointed executive director in 1989 and became CEO in 1990.

Beyond his business career, Al Ghurair served as Speaker of the Federal National Council (FNC) of the United Arab Emirates from 2007 to 2011, representing a significant political appointment. As Chairman of Mashreq, he oversees a financial institution that was the first in the Middle East to launch ATMs, debit and credit cards, travellers' cheques, consumer loans, and point-of-sale terminals. He also chairs Al Ghurair Investments and the Arab Business Angels Network. Forbes estimated his net worth at $2.6 billion as of 2022. His philanthropic work includes support for UNICEF, UNESCO, and the UAE Disabled Sports Federation, and in 2015 he pledged one-third of his group's assets to the Abdulla Al Ghurair Foundation for Education.
achievements: Speaker of UAE Federal National Council (2007-2011)
CEO of Mashreq Bank (1990-present)
Chairman of Dubai Chamber of Commerce
Pledged one-third of assets to education foundation in 2015

figures.name: Abdul Rahman Saif Al Ghurair
figures.title: Chairman of Al Ghurair Group
figures.roleDescription: Chairman of the Saif Al Ghurair Group
figures.significance: Current chairman of the Al Ghurair Group and grandson of founder Ahmad Al Ghurair, representing the third generation leadership of the family business.
figures.biography:
Abdul Rahman Saif Al Ghurair is the current chairman of the Al Ghurair Group and is one of the grandsons of the group's founder Ahmad Al Ghurair. He represents the third generation of Al Ghurair family leadership in the business empire. As chairman, he oversees the group's diverse operations across manufacturing, real estate, and investments.

Abdul Rahman previously served as chairman of the Dubai Chamber of Commerce and Industry, demonstrating his involvement in the broader Dubai business community. Under his leadership alongside his brother Majid as CEO, the Al Ghurair Group continues to operate as one of the largest diversified family business groups in the Middle East, with major subsidiaries in petrochemicals, aluminium, steel, packaging, and real estate development.
achievements: Chairman of Al Ghurair Group
Former Chairman of Dubai Chamber of Commerce and Industry

figures.name: Majid Saif Al Ghurair
figures.title: CEO of Al Ghurair Group
figures.roleDescription: Chief Executive Officer of the Saif Al Ghurair Group
figures.significance: Current CEO of the Al Ghurair Group and grandson of founder Ahmad Al Ghurair, leading the operational management of one of the Middle East's largest diversified business groups.
figures.biography:
Majid Saif Al Ghurair serves as the Chief Executive Officer of the Al Ghurair Group and is one of the grandsons of the group's founder Ahmad Al Ghurair. He graduated from Al Ain University with a bachelor's degree in accounting and represents the third generation of family leadership in the business empire. As CEO, he is responsible for the operational management and strategic direction of the group's diverse portfolio.

Majid holds several prominent board positions, including chairman of Drake & Scull International and the Middle East Council of Shopping Centers, and serves as a board member of Investcorp Bank BSC. He is also a member and Young Global Leader of the World Economic Forum, reflecting his standing in the regional and international business community. Under his leadership, the Al Ghurair Group continues to operate major businesses in petrochemicals, aluminium, packaging, and real estate development across the Middle East, North Africa, Europe, and North America.
achievements: CEO of Al Ghurair Group
Chairman of Drake & Scull International
Chairman of Middle East Council of Shopping Centers
Board member of Investcorp Bank BSC
Young Global Leader of World Economic Forum

figures.name: Badr Al Ghurair
figures.title: CEO of Cars Taxi
figures.roleDescription: Chief Executive Officer of Dubai's largest taxi fleet provider
figures.significance: CEO of Cars Taxi, Dubai's largest taxi fleet provider, and son of Mashreq Bank founder Abdulla bin Ahmad Al Ghurair.
figures.biography:
Badr Al Ghurair is the Chief Executive Officer of Cars Taxi, which operates as the largest taxi fleet provider in Dubai and is part of Al Ghurair's mobility sector. In his role, Badr oversees the ongoing operations of all divisions within the company. Under his leadership, Cars Taxi became notable as the first taxi company in the UAE to incorporate hybrid taxis into their fleet, demonstrating innovation in sustainable transportation.

Badr is the son of Abdulla bin Ahmad Al Ghurair, the founder and chairman of Mashreq Bank, positioning him as part of the third generation of the Al Ghurair business family. His role in the mobility sector represents the family's diversification beyond their traditional areas of banking, construction, and manufacturing into modern transportation services.
achievements: CEO of Dubai's largest taxi fleet provider
First to incorporate hybrid taxis in UAE taxi fleet

figures.name: Abdullah Al Rostamani
figures.title: Co-Founder of AW Rostamani Group
figures.roleDescription: Founding patriarch and co-founder of the family business empire
figures.significance: Co-founded AW Rostamani Group in 1954, establishing one of Dubai's major merchant family businesses.
figures.biography:
Abdullah Al Rostamani was one of two sons of a local pearl trader who co-founded the AW Rostamani business empire. In 1951, he and his brother Abdul Wahid Hassan set up their family business on the shore of Dubai Creek. The brothers were inspired by their passion for reading and opened Dubai's first bookshop, Al Ahliya Library, in 1954, importing sought-after books from Egypt, Syria, and Lebanon through Gulf Air via Bahrain.

Abdullah played a crucial role in establishing what would become AW Rostamani Group, officially founded in 1954. The business began as a modest trading operation but would eventually grow into a major diversified conglomerate operating across seven sectors including automotive, real estate, logistics, retail, lighting, travel and agritech, employing over 4,000 workers.
achievements: Co-founded Dubai's first bookshop, Al Ahliya Library, in 1954
Co-established AW Rostamani Group in 1954
Pioneered book importing business in early Dubai

figures.name: Abdul Wahid Al Rostamani
figures.title: Co-Founder of AW Rostamani Group
figures.roleDescription: Founding patriarch and co-founder of the family business empire
figures.significance: Co-founded AW Rostamani Group in 1954, establishing one of Dubai's major merchant family businesses.
figures.biography:
Abdul Wahid Al Rostamani was one of two sons of a local pearl trader who co-founded the AW Rostamani business empire alongside his brother Abdullah Hassan. In 1951, the brothers set up their family business on the shore of Dubai Creek, driven by their entrepreneurial spirit and passion for reading. They opened Dubai's first bookshop, Al Ahliya Library, in 1954, importing books from Egypt, Syria, and Lebanon.

Abdul Wahid's vision helped establish what became AW Rostamani Group in 1954, which grew from a small trading operation into a major diversified conglomerate. The company now operates across seven diverse sectors and employs over 4,000 workers, making it one of Dubai's significant family-owned business groups.
achievements: Co-founded Dubai's first bookshop, Al Ahliya Library, in 1954
Co-established AW Rostamani Group in 1954
Built foundation for major diversified business conglomerate

figures.name: Khalid Abdul Wahid Al Rostamani
figures.title: Chairman of AW Rostamani Group
figures.roleDescription: Current family patriarch and business leader
figures.significance: Current Chairman of AW Rostamani Group, leading the family's diversified business empire.
figures.biography:
Khalid Abdul Wahid Al Rostamani serves as the Chairman of AW Rostamani Group, representing the second generation leadership of the family business empire. As the son of co-founder Abdul Wahid Al Rostamani, he has inherited and continued to develop the business legacy established by his father and uncle in the 1950s.

Under his leadership, AW Rostamani Group has maintained its position as a major diversified conglomerate in the UAE, operating across seven sectors including automotive, real estate, logistics, retail, lighting, travel and agritech. The group employs over 4,000 workers and continues to expand its business operations regionally and internationally.
achievements: Leads major UAE diversified business conglomerate
Continues family business legacy into second generation
Oversees operations across seven business sectors

figures.name: Amina Al Rustamani
figures.title: CEO of AWR Properties and COO of AW Rostamani Group
figures.roleDescription: Prominent businesswoman and senior executive in family business
figures.significance: Leading Emirati businesswoman who ranked among the world's most powerful Arab women and serves in senior roles across the family business empire.
figures.biography:
Dr. Amina Al Rustamani is a prominent Emirati businesswoman who currently serves as Director and Board Member of AW Rostamani Group and Chief Executive Officer of AWR Properties. She earned her bachelor's, master's and doctoral degrees in electrical engineering from The George Washington University in 1993, 1996, and 2001, respectively. She started her career in Dubai in 2001 as an electrical engineer with TECOM Investments (now TECOM Group), where she was involved in the development of Dubai Design District and Dubai Wholesale City.

In February 2018, Al Rustamani left TECOM Group to take up the position as Chief Operating Officer of the AW Rostamani Group while also serving as CEO of AWR Properties, the real estate arm of the family business. She has achieved significant recognition for her business leadership, ranking number 9 in CEO Middle East's fifth annual list of the world's most powerful Arab women in 2015 and being picked as the Advertising Person of the Year by Dubai Lynx for 2015. She was also listed as number 20 in Forbes 'Middle East Power Businesswomen 2021.'

Beyond the family business, she serves on multiple high-profile boards including Dubai Healthcare City Authority, Noor Bank, and HSBC Bank Middle East. Her previous board positions included Dubai Media Incorporated, National Media Council, and Emirates Central Cooling Systems Corporation (Empower), and she served as Chairperson of the Dubai Design and Fashion Council.
achievements: Ranked #9 in CEO Middle East's most powerful Arab women list (2015)
Named Advertising Person of the Year by Dubai Lynx (2015)
Listed #20 in Forbes Middle East Power Businesswomen 2021
Earned doctoral degree in electrical engineering from George Washington University
Former Group CEO of TECOM Group

figures.name: Isa Bin Abdullatif Al Serkal
figures.title: British Residency Agent
figures.roleDescription: British Native Residency Agent in Sharjah during the early-mid 20th century
figures.significance: Served as the key British representative and intermediary in Sharjah, playing a crucial role in regional politics and negotiations including the establishment of Sharjah Airport.
figures.biography:
Isa bin Abdullatif Al Serkal served as the British Residency Agent in Sharjah during a critical period of regional development in the early-to-mid 20th century. He acted as the primary intermediary between the British government and local rulers, representing British interests while managing complex tribal and political relationships. His role was particularly significant during the turbulent period involving Abdulrahman bin Muhammad Al Shamsi, the headman of Al Heera, when Al Serkal's cousin, Ibrahim bin Rajab, was killed by a shot fired in Sharjah on 11 October 1925. The attack was believed to have been aimed at Al Serkal himself, highlighting the dangerous nature of his position.

Al Serkal played a crucial role in the negotiations for the establishment of Sharjah Airport in the 1930s. He was a co-signatory to the historic agreement made on 22 June 1932 between the British government and Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi, the Ruler of Sharjah, which established the first airport in the Trucial States. The agreement stipulated that Imperial Airways would only deal with the Sheikh through the Residency Agent, emphasizing Al Serkal's central role in this groundbreaking aviation development. His position required him to balance British imperial interests with local sensitivities, often in challenging circumstances involving tribal conflicts, debt disputes, and territorial disagreements among the various rulers of the Trucial Coast.
achievements: Co-signed the historic 1932 agreement establishing Sharjah Airport, the first airport in the Trucial States
Served as key British intermediary in complex tribal and political negotiations
Represented British subjects' interests in debt disputes involving local merchants

figures.name: Mukhtar Ahmed Ansari
figures.title: Surgeon and Political Leader
figures.roleDescription: Early president of Indian National Congress and Muslim League
figures.significance: Served as president of both the Indian National Congress (1927) and Muslim League (1918, 1920) during India's independence movement.
figures.biography: Mukhtar Ahmed Ansari (1880-1936) was a prominent surgeon and political leader during India's independence movement. He served as president of the Muslim League in both 1918 and 1920, demonstrating his early leadership in Muslim political representation. Later, he became president of the Indian National Congress in 1927, showing his broader nationalist credentials and ability to bridge communal divides during a critical period in Indian politics. His dual leadership roles in both major political organizations of the time made him a significant figure in the struggle for Indian independence. As a medical professional, he combined his surgical career with political activism, representing the educated Muslim elite's participation in the nationalist movement.
achievements: President of Muslim League (1918, 1920)
President of Indian National Congress (1927)
Prominent surgeon and medical professional

figures.name: Mohammad Hamid Ansari
figures.title: Vice President of India
figures.roleDescription: Diplomat and Vice President of India (2007-2017)
figures.significance: Served as Vice President of India for two terms (2007-2017) with the longest vice presidential tenure in Indian history.
figures.biography: Mohammad Hamid Ansari was born on April 1, 1937, in Kolkata to Mohammad Abdul Aziz Ansari and Aasiya Begum. Though his ancestral home is in Yusufpur, Ghazipur, Uttar Pradesh, he spent his formative years in Kolkata. He comes from a family of Ansari sheikhs descended from the Sufi saint Abdullah Ansari of Herat, and is the grand-nephew of former Indian National Congress president Mukhtar Ahmed Ansari. Joining the Indian Foreign Service in 1961, Ansari served as ambassador to several countries including the UAE (1976-1980), Australia (1985-1989), Afghanistan (1989-1990), Iran (1990-1992), and Saudi Arabia (1995-1999). He also served as India's permanent representative to the United Nations from 1993 to 1995. After his diplomatic career, he became vice-chancellor of Aligarh Muslim University (2000-2002) and chairman of the National Commission for Minorities (2006-2007). Elected Vice President of India on August 10, 2007, he was re-elected in 2012, serving two full terms until 2017. His tenure is tied with Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan for the longest vice presidential tenure in Indian history.
achievements: Vice President of India (2007-2017)
Ambassador to multiple countries including Australia, Iran, and Saudi Arabia
Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations (1993-1995)
Vice-Chancellor of Aligarh Muslim University (2000-2002)
Longest serving Vice President in Indian history

figures.name: Mukhtar Ansari
figures.title: Politician and Gangster
figures.roleDescription: Member of Legislative Assembly from Mau constituency
figures.significance: Five-time MLA from Mau constituency who combined criminal activities with electoral politics in Uttar Pradesh.
figures.biography: Mukhtar Ansari (June 30, 1963 – March 28, 2024) was an Indian gangster, convicted murderer, and politician based in Uttar Pradesh. His paternal grandfather was Mukhtar Ahmed Ansari, the surgeon and early president of the Indian National Congress and Muslim League. His father was Subhan Ullah Ansari, a Communist party leader. The family claims patrilineal lineage from the 11th century Sufi saint Abdullah Ansari of Herat in Afghanistan. Ansari entered politics around 1995 through a student union and became an MLA in 1996, eventually being elected five times from the Mau constituency. He joined the Bahujan Samaj Party in 2007 along with his brother Afzal, but was expelled in 2010 after continued involvement in criminal activities. He then formed his own party, Quami Ekta Dal, in 2010 before rejoining the BSP in 2016. Throughout his political career, he faced numerous criminal cases and spent considerable time in jail from 2005 onwards. He died of a heart attack on March 28, 2024, while in prison at age 60, with his family alleging he was poisoned.
achievements: Five-time MLA from Mau constituency
Founded Quami Ekta Dal political party
Contested Lok Sabha elections from Varanasi against Narendra Modi

figures.name: Aziz Ansari
figures.title: Actor and Comedian
figures.roleDescription: American actor, comedian, and filmmaker
figures.significance: First Asian American actor to win a Golden Globe for acting on television for his role in Master of None.
figures.biography: Aziz Ismail Ansari was born on February 23, 1983, in Columbia, South Carolina, to Indian immigrants from Tamil Nadu. His parents are Tamil Muslims, though Ansari himself is non-religious. He graduated from NYU Stern School of Business in 2004 with a Bachelor of Business degree in marketing. Ansari began performing comedy in New York City while a student, frequently performing at the Upright Citizens Brigade Theatre. He co-created and starred in the MTV sketch comedy show Human Giant before landing his breakthrough role as Tom Haverford on NBC's Parks and Recreation (2009-2015). In 2015, he co-created and starred in Netflix's critically acclaimed series Master of None, for which he won several awards including two Emmys and a Golden Globe - making him the first Asian American actor to win a Golden Globe for acting on television. As a stand-up comedian, he has released multiple comedy specials and continues to tour. His first book, Modern Romance: An Investigation, was released in 2015, and he was included in Time's 100 most influential people list in 2016.
achievements: First Asian American to win Golden Globe for TV acting
Co-creator and star of Netflix's Master of None
Two-time Emmy winner
Author of bestselling book Modern Romance
Time 100 most influential people (2016)

figures.name: Yassamin Ansari
figures.title: U.S. Representative
figures.roleDescription: U.S. Representative for Arizona's 3rd congressional district
figures.significance: First Iranian American elected to public office in Arizona and youngest female member of Congress.
figures.biography: Yassamin Ansari was born on April 7, 1992, in Seattle, Washington, to parents who immigrated to the United States after fleeing Iran following the Islamic Revolution. She grew up in Scottsdale, Arizona, and attended Chaparral High School, where she organized with the Arizona Democratic Party for Barack Obama's 2008 campaign. She attended Stanford University, receiving a bachelor's degree in international relations, and later earned a master's degree from St. John's College, Cambridge as a Gates Cambridge Scholar. After graduation, she worked in the UN Secretary-General's office under Ban Ki-moon and António Guterres, focusing on climate policy including the Paris Agreement. She was elected to the Phoenix City Council in 2021, becoming the youngest person elected to the council and the first Iranian American elected to public office in Arizona. During her tenure, she created an Office of Heat Response and Mitigation and promoted climate action initiatives. In 2024, she was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives for Arizona's 3rd congressional district, becoming the youngest female member of Congress.
achievements: First Iranian American elected to public office in Arizona
Youngest female member of Congress
Gates Cambridge Scholar
Worked on Paris Agreement at UN
Created Office of Heat Response and Mitigation in Phoenix

figures.name: Amna bint Abdullah Al Dahak Al Shamsi
figures.nameAr: آمنة بنت عبد الله الضحاك الشامسي
figures.title: Minister of Climate Change and Environment
figures.roleDescription: Current UAE government minister and former COVID-19 spokesperson
figures.significance: She serves as the UAE Minister of Climate Change and Environment since January 2024 and was the government spokesperson during the COVID-19 pandemic.
figures.biography:
Amna bint Abdullah Al Dahak Al Shamsi is an Emirati politician who has served as the Minister of Climate Change and Environment since 6 January 2024. She holds a PhD in Computer Engineering from Khalifa University, and both bachelor's and master's degrees in Computer Science from the University of Sharjah. She previously served as the spokesperson for the UAE Government during the COVID-19 pandemic and chaired the organizing committees for the International Junior Science Olympiad 2021 and the International Biology Olympiad 2023.

Prior to her ministerial appointment, she held the position of Assistant Undersecretary for the Care and Capacity Building Sector at the Ministry of Education since July 2016, and served as Director of the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Department at the Ministry of Education. She has been a member of several committees, including the Digital Transformation Committee, the National Family Prevention Committee, and the Gulf Gifted Council. She chairs several national committees, including the Child Protection Council in the Educational Environment, the National Committee for Green Education, and the National Committee for Creative Arts in Education.
achievements: Appointed Minister of Climate Change and Environment in 2024
Served as UAE Government spokesperson during COVID-19 pandemic
Chaired organizing committees for International Science Olympiads
PhD in Computer Engineering from Khalifa University

figures.name: Maitha Salem Al Shamsi
figures.title: Minister of State
figures.roleDescription: UAE government minister and prominent women's rights advocate
figures.significance: She serves as Minister of State in the UAE and was ranked 13th on Forbes Middle East's list of most powerful Arab women in the Middle East.
figures.biography:
Maitha Salem Al Shamsi is a politician and government minister in the United Arab Emirates who serves as Minister of State. She completed her Bachelor of Arts in 1980 from King Saud University in Saudi Arabia and her master's degree in 1988 from Alexandria University in Egypt. She also completed a post doctorate in Sociology and has written several books including 'Expatriate Migration to the Gulf Cooperation Council States: Current Problems and Future Prospects', 'the Mother of the Nation: Fatima bint Mubarak', and 'Principles and Accomplishments'.

In 2008 she joined the Federal Government of the United Arab Emirates as the Minister of State and helped establish the Sorbonne University in Abu Dhabi. She has held numerous leadership positions including serving on the board of trustees of Abu Dhabi University, sitting on UNESCO's Scientific Committee for the Arab States on Education and Scientific Research, and serving as chairperson of the Marriage Fund until February 2016. She currently serves as chairperson of the Family Development Foundation, the General Women's Union in United Arab Emirates, and the Supreme Council for Motherhood and Childhood. In 2017, she pledged seven million dollars to support Rohingya refugees at a United Nations conference in Geneva.
achievements: Ranked 13th on Forbes Middle East's most powerful Arab women list
Awarded Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum Award for Arab Management in 2003
Helped establish Sorbonne University in Abu Dhabi in 2008
Pledged $7 million for Rohingya refugee support in 2017
Chairperson of multiple UAE national organizations

figures.name: Abdulrahman bin Muhammad Al Shamsi
figures.title: Sheikh of Al Heera
figures.roleDescription: Influential and controversial tribal leader in early 20th century Trucial States
figures.significance: He was a highly influential and divisive figure in regional politics during the early 20th century, described by British officials as 'a stormy petrel of the Trucial Coast, a man feared by everyone in and around Sharjah'.
figures.biography:
Abdulrahman bin Muhammad Al Shamsi was the Sheikh, or head man, of the township of Al Heera, today a suburb of Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. A highly influential and often divisive figure in regional politics during the early 20th century in the Trucial States, he was frequently at odds with other rulers and often in trouble with the British administration. By 1920, facing enormous debts of Rs 21,560 from his town's struggling pearling fleet, he led a desperate attack on Ajman on 15 June 1920, occupying its fort for six days before being forced out by British intervention.

His conflicts led to multiple exiles, including a period with the Shihuh of the Rus Al Jibal mountains and later a four-year exile to Aden (returning after three years) following accusations of involvement in the 1925 killing of Ibrahim bin Rajab in Sharjah. Despite his turbulent relationships, Al Shamsi played a crucial role in the establishment of Sharjah Airport, with his influential backing among Bedouin tribes being instrumental in Sultan bin Saqr Al Qasimi's successful negotiations with the British for the airport in 1932. He ruled Al Heera until his death on 10 August 1942, when the township reverted to rule by Sharjah.
achievements: Instrumental in concluding negotiations for Sharjah Airport establishment
Maintained significant influence among Bedouin tribes including the Manasir and Bani Qitab
Survived multiple political conflicts and exiles while maintaining leadership of Al Heera

figures.name: Muhammad Sa'id al-Suwaidi
figures.title: Sheikh
figures.roleDescription: Islamic scholar, poet, and mosque Imam
figures.significance: A prominent Iraqi Islamic scholar and leader of the Sufi Naqshbandi Order in Baghdad during the Ottoman period.
figures.biography:
Sheikh Muhammad Sa'id al-Suwaidi was born in Karkh, Baghdad, in 1728 to the Iraqi al-Suwaidi family, which was famous for its knowledge, jurisprudence, and literature. His father, Sheikh Abdullah bin Hussein al-Suwaidi, was the author of many books, including 'Al-Nafha al-Miskiyya' on the Meccan journey. He studied under his father as well as other scholars such as Sheikh Abd al-Qadir al-Makki al-Harithi and Sheikh Ali al-Ansari, and would temporarily visit Cairo's madrasas.

As a member and leader of the Sufi Naqshbandi Order in Baghdad, Ottoman Iraq, he became a significant religious figure. His house in Karkh was a notable landmark in Baghdad and was later inherited by his son, Muhammad Amin, who used it as a madrasa where he would teach. The upper floor overlooking the road served as a majlis. When the structure weakened, it was renovated and became known as al-Suwaidi Madrasa, remaining one of the famous schools in Karkh until Muhammad Amin's death in 1830. He died in 1808 and was buried in the Sheikh Ma'ruf Cemetery.
achievements: Leader of the Sufi Naqshbandi Order in Baghdad
Established influential madrasa that operated until 1830
Part of scholarly family tradition in Islamic jurisprudence and literature

figures.name: Salemin bin Nasir Al-Suwaidi
figures.title: Chief
figures.roleDescription: Chief of the Sudan tribe (Suwaidi) in Al Bidda
figures.significance: Led the Sudan tribe in Al Bidda and dealt with British authorities during incidents involving pirates and territorial disputes.
figures.biography:
Salemin bin Nasir Al-Suwaidi served as chief of the Sudan tribe (Suwaidi) in Al Bidda during the mid-19th century. In November 1839, when an outlaw from Abu Dhabi named Ghuleta took refuge in Al Bidda, British naval commander A. H. Nott demanded that Al-Suwaidi take Ghuleta into custody and warned him of consequences in case of non-compliance. Al-Suwaidi obliged the British request in February 1840 and also arrested the pirate Jasim bin Jabir and his associates.

Despite his compliance, the British demanded a fine of 300 German krones in compensation for damages incurred by pirates off the coast of Al Bidda. In February 1841, British naval squadrons arrived and threatened consequences if he declined to pay. Al-Suwaidi ultimately declined on the basis that he was uninvolved in bin Jabir's actions. On 26 February, the British fired on Al Bidda, striking a fort and several houses. Al-Suwaidi then paid the fine in full following threats of further action by the British.
achievements: Led the Sudan tribe during British colonial period
Managed tribal relations with British authorities
Maintained tribal leadership during territorial conflicts

figures.name: Sultan bin Nasir Al Suwaidi
figures.title: Sheikh
figures.roleDescription: Sheikh of the Sudan tribe and father-in-law of Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan
figures.significance: Connected the Al Suwaidi family to the ruling Al Nahyan family through his daughter's marriage to Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan.
figures.biography:
Sultan bin Nasir Al Suwaidi served as Sheikh of the Sudan tribe during the late 19th century and played a significant role in tribal politics of the region. He became closely connected to the ruling Al Nahyan family when Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan married his daughter, making him the father-in-law of one of Abu Dhabi's most influential rulers.

Sultan bin Nasir was instrumental in Zayed's political schemes, particularly the plan to establish a fort at Al Zorah in Ajman in 1895. It was with the Sudan tribe under Sultan bin Nasir that Zayed conceived this idea to consolidate and build a bridgehead into the northern emirates. In 1897, a section of the Sudan under Sultan bin Nasser requested permission to settle Al Zorah with Zayed's support, which was initially granted by the British Resident, though the scheme was later abandoned due to opposition from other rulers.
achievements: Sheikh of the Sudan tribe
Father-in-law to Sheikh Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan
Key ally in Abu Dhabi's northern expansion plans

figures.name: Salimayn bin Nasir al-Suwaidi
figures.title: Headman
figures.roleDescription: Headman of the Sudan tribe in Bida'a, Qatar
figures.significance: Led the Sudan tribe in Qatar during conflicts with British forces over piracy accusations.
figures.biography:
Salimayn bin Nasir al-Suwaidi served as headman of the Sudan tribe in Bida'a (present-day Doha) during the early 19th century. In February 1841, he became involved in a significant incident when the Sudan were suspected of harboring the pirate Jasim bin Jabir, known as Raqraqi, in Bida'a. This led to British naval intervention as they sought to punish those they believed were supporting piracy in the Persian Gulf.

When a British squadron arrived to confront him, Salimayn initially resisted their demands. However, after the British fired several shots at the town as a show of force, he was compelled to comply with their ultimatum. He paid a substantial fine in cash and jewelry to the British authorities and handed over Raqraqi's ship, which was subsequently set on fire by the British forces. This incident represents the challenges faced by tribal leaders in Qatar during the period of British maritime dominance in the Gulf.
achievements: Headman of Sudan tribe in Qatar
Navigated tribal leadership during British colonial pressures
Managed resolution of piracy accusations against his tribe

figures.name: Ahmad bin Khalifah Al Suwaidi
figures.title: Sheikh
figures.roleDescription: Political leader and opponent in Abu Dhabi succession disputes
figures.significance: Played a key role in Abu Dhabi political affairs and succession disputes in 1928.
figures.biography:
Sheikh Ahmad bin Khalifah Al Suwaidi was a prominent political figure in Abu Dhabi during the early 20th century, particularly influential in matters of succession and tribal politics. He represented the Sudan tribe's interests in the highest levels of Abu Dhabi's political hierarchy and often served as a voice for tribal concerns in the ruling family's deliberations.

In 1928, Ahmad bin Khalifah Al Suwaidi played a crucial role in Abu Dhabi political affairs when he led the opposition to Shakhbut bin Sultan becoming ruler. When this succession was proposed to the family and leading notables, Al Suwaidi organized resistance and instead supported Hazza bin Sultan for the position. His opposition demonstrated the significant political influence that the Sudan tribe, and the Al Suwaidi family in particular, wielded in Abu Dhabi's internal affairs during this critical period of the emirate's development.
achievements: Led opposition in Abu Dhabi succession dispute of 1928
Represented Sudan tribe interests in high-level politics
Influential voice in Abu Dhabi's political decision-making

figures.name: Salama bint Butti Al Qubaisi
figures.title: Sheikha
figures.roleDescription: Wife of Abu Dhabi ruler and matriarch of the Al Nahyan ruling dynasty
figures.significance: She was the wife of Sheikh Sultan bin Zayed Al Nahyan and mother of Sheikhs Shakhbut and Zayed, who ruled Abu Dhabi and founded the UAE.
figures.biography:
Sheikha Salama bint Butti Al Qubaisi was from the prominent Al Qubaisi Bedouin tribe from Liwa, who also settled Khor Al Adaid. She was originally from Mezairaa in Liwa. She married Sheikh Sultan bin Zayed bin Khalifa Al Nahyan, who became Ruler of the Emirate of Abu Dhabi from 1922.

After her husband's assassination in 1927, she demonstrated remarkable leadership by taking her son Zayed from Abu Dhabi to Al Ain for safety. She made her sons swear an oath that they would not kill or fight against each other, helping to maintain family unity during a turbulent period. Her sons included Sheikh Shakhbut (who ruled Abu Dhabi from 1928 to 1966), Sheikh Zayed (who ruled Abu Dhabi from 1966 to 2004 and became the first president of the UAE), and Hazza bin Sultan, who served as the Ruler's Representative of the Western Region.

In early 1955, her sons Zayed, Hazza and Shakhbut helped arrange her return to Abu Dhabi on board a Gulf Air plane. She died in October 1970, during the time when her son Zayed was the Ruler of Abu Dhabi. Her legacy is commemorated through the Sheikha Salama Mosque, which was named after her.
achievements: Mother of two Abu Dhabi rulers including UAE founding president Sheikh Zayed
Maintained family unity after husband's assassination
Matriarch of the Al Nahyan ruling dynasty
Commemorated with Sheikha Salama Mosque

figures.name: Khaled Abdulla Al Qubaisi
figures.title: CEO and Racing Driver
figures.roleDescription: Former CEO of Real Estate & Infrastructure Investments at Mubadala Investment Company and professional racing driver
figures.significance: He is a prominent Emirati businessman who led major investments at Mubadala and achieved international success in motorsport, including winning the Dubai 24 Hour race twice.
figures.biography:
Khaled Abdulla Al Qubaisi was born on December 22, 1975, and is an accomplished Emirati businessman and racing driver. He holds a Master of Science in Project Management from George Washington University and a bachelor's degree in finance and operations management from Boston University. He was awarded the CFA designation in 2003, demonstrating his financial expertise.

Al Qubaisi served as CEO of Mubadala's Real Estate & Infrastructure Investments platform from 2021 to 2024, overseeing major investment portfolios. Prior to this, he held various senior positions including Chief Human Capital Officer at Mubadala, Chief Investment Officer at International Capital, and Head of Corporate Finance & Business Development at the National Bank of Abu Dhabi. He currently serves as Chairman of the National Central Cooling Company (Tabreed) and sits on numerous corporate boards.

In motorsport, Al Qubaisi has achieved remarkable success in international grand touring races since the early 2010s. His biggest accomplishments include overall victories at the 2012 and 2013 Dubai 24 Hour races, driving a Black Falcon Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG GT3. He also won the Yas Marina 12-Hour Race in 2013 and competed three times in the 24 Hours of Le Mans, with his best finish being 19th overall in 2014. He is married with three children, including two daughters who are also racing drivers.
achievements: CEO of Mubadala Real Estate & Infrastructure Investments (2021-2024)
Overall winner of Dubai 24 Hour race (2012, 2013)
Winner of Yas Marina 12-Hour Race (2013)
Chairman of National Central Cooling Company (Tabreed)
CFA designation holder

figures.name: Amna Al Qubaisi
figures.title: Racing Driver
figures.roleDescription: Emirati female racing driver
figures.significance: She is a notable Emirati female racing driver, representing the new generation of women in motorsport in the UAE.
figures.biography:
Amna Al Qubaisi, born in 2000, is an Emirati female racing driver who represents the emerging generation of women in motorsport in the United Arab Emirates. She is the daughter of Khaled Abdulla Al Qubaisi, the prominent businessman and racing driver who achieved international success in grand touring races.

As a member of a family with strong motorsport traditions, Amna has followed in her father's footsteps by pursuing a career in racing. She has a sister, Hamda, who is also a racing driver, demonstrating that the Al Qubaisi family has produced multiple generations of motorsport talent. Her involvement in racing reflects the growing participation of Emirati women in traditionally male-dominated sports and the UAE's broader efforts to promote gender equality in various fields.

Amna's racing career represents part of the broader development of motorsport in the UAE and the increasing visibility of female drivers in the region. Her participation in racing helps inspire other young Emirati women to pursue careers in motorsport and other challenging fields.
achievements: Professional racing driver
Part of motorsport family legacy

figures.name: Khadem al-Qubaisi
figures.title: Former IPIC Managing Director
figures.roleDescription: Former managing director of International Petroleum Investment Company (IPIC)
figures.significance: He was a senior executive at IPIC who became involved in the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal and was arrested in 2016.
figures.biography:
Khadem al-Qubaisi served as the managing director of the International Petroleum Investment Company (IPIC), a major Abu Dhabi-based sovereign wealth fund focused on energy investments. He held a senior position in the UAE's investment landscape and was responsible for managing significant financial portfolios on behalf of the Emirates.

Al-Qubaisi became prominently known due to his involvement in the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, one of the world's largest financial fraud cases. He was arrested in 2016 as authorities investigated the complex web of financial transactions and alleged corruption surrounding the Malaysian sovereign wealth fund. Following the scandal's exposure, IPIC was folded into Aabar Investments as part of restructuring efforts.

According to reports, al-Qubaisi blamed UAE authorities, including Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan, for using him as a scapegoat in the affair. His case highlighted the complex relationships between sovereign wealth funds, international finance, and the challenges of oversight in large-scale investment operations.
achievements: Managing Director of IPIC
Senior executive in UAE sovereign wealth fund sector

figures.name: Mahmoud Pasha Hammadi
figures.title: Pasha and Founder
figures.roleDescription: Founder of Nag Hammadi city and major landholder
figures.significance: Founded the city of Nag Hammadi in Egypt and was a prominent opponent of British colonial rule.
figures.biography:
Mahmoud Pasha Hammadi was a member of the Hammadi family in Sohag, Egypt, who founded the town of Nag Hammadi in the 19th century. The city was established on the site of older villages Ansan and al-Luaqi and was named after its founder. He was a major landholder in Sohag and became known for his strong opposition to British rule in Egypt beginning in 1882.

As a pasha, Mahmoud held significant political and social influence in Upper Egypt. His opposition to British colonial administration made him a notable figure in Egyptian resistance during the early period of British occupation. The city he founded, Nag Hammadi, would later become famous for the discovery of ancient Gnostic texts in 1945, though this occurred long after his time.
achievements: Founded the city of Nag Hammadi
Led opposition to British rule in Egypt from 1882

figures.name: Sa'dun Hammadi
figures.title: Foreign Minister and Prime Minister of Iraq
figures.roleDescription: Iraqi politician, writer, and economist who served in senior government positions
figures.significance: Served as Iraq's Foreign Minister for nearly a decade and briefly as Prime Minister, playing key roles in oil nationalization and international diplomacy.
figures.biography:
Sa'dun Hammadi was born to a Shi'ite family in Karbala on 22 June 1930. He pursued higher education, obtaining a Master's degree in economics from the American University of Beirut and a PhD in economics from the University of Wisconsin–Madison in 1956. Hammadi joined the Ba'ath Party in 1949 and is credited with introducing Ba'athism to Iraq, becoming believed to be the first Iraqi member of the party.

Hammadi's political career began in earnest after the 1963 coup, when he was appointed Minister of Agrarian Reform. He later became head of the Iraq National Oil Company in 1968 and Minister of Oil in 1970, overseeing the historic nationalization of Iraq's oil industry in 1972. From 1974 to 1983, he served as Iraq's Foreign Minister, representing the country during crucial periods including the Iran-Iraq War and conducting diplomatic missions worldwide.

In March 1991, following the Gulf War, Hammadi was appointed Prime Minister by Saddam Hussein but was forced out by September due to his reformist views. He then served as Speaker of the National Assembly from 1996 until the 2003 U.S. invasion. After being detained by American forces for nine months, he was released in 2004 and eventually settled in Qatar, where he engaged in cultural activities until his death from liver cancer in Germany on 14 March 2007.
achievements: Oversaw Iraq's oil nationalization in 1972
Served as Foreign Minister for nearly a decade (1974-1983)
Introduced Ba'athism to Iraq as first Iraqi party member
Authored numerous cultural and political works

figures.name: Areej Al Hammadi
figures.title: Football Player
figures.roleDescription: Emirati football player
figures.significance: Notable Emirati female football player representing the Al Hammadi family in sports.
figures.biography:
Areej Al Hammadi is an Emirati football player born in 1986. As a member of the Al Hammadi family, she represents the modern generation of the family's involvement in sports and athletics in the United Arab Emirates.

Her career in football demonstrates the family's continued prominence in contemporary UAE society, extending beyond traditional business and political spheres into sports and athletic achievement.

figures.name: Juma al Majid
figures.title: Founder and Chairman of Juma Al Majid Holding Group
figures.roleDescription: Emirati businessman, political adviser, and philanthropist
figures.significance: Founder of one of the UAE's most prominent business conglomerates and major philanthropist in education and culture.
figures.biography:
Juma al Majid was born in Al Shandagah, Dubai, in 1930 to a pearl diver's family. He received early education in Qur'an and Arabic at local kuttāb classes. At age 15, his uncle Ahmad Majid Al Ghurair asked him to work at his shop in Deira, eventually opening a second shop for him. In 1950, he established Juma Al Majid Holding Group, initially selling fabric before switching to air conditioners. He partnered with Mohammad Al Qaz, forming a successful trading operation that included the Switzerland-Dubai-India gold trade.

By the 1970s, his company had grown to be the second largest distributor of General Electric products, selling up to 700,000 air conditioners per year. After UAE Federation in 1971, he and Al Qaz opened a Pepsi factory in Dubai and expanded into various sectors including automotive, real estate, and hospitality. Throughout his career, he acquired franchise rights for international brands like Samsung, Hyundai, and Hitachi. Al Majid also held key positions including Chairman of Dubai Economic Council and Vice Chairman of the UAE Central Bank.

As a philanthropist, he established numerous educational institutions including the Islamic and Arabic Studies College in Dubai in 1987, and founded the Juma Al Majid Center for Culture and Heritage in 1989. He co-founded Dubai's first charitable society and established the National Charity Schools in 1983 to provide free education to poor expatriate children.
achievements: Ranked among the richest Arabs in the world in 2016
Founded Juma Al Majid Holding Group in 1950
Established Islamic and Arabic Studies College in Dubai (1987)
Founded Juma Al Majid Center for Culture and Heritage (1989)
King Faisal International Award for service to Islam (1999)
UAE Pioneers Award (2015)
Medal of Culture & Creativity - UAE's highest cultural honor (2024)

figures.name: Ahmad Majid Al Ghurair
figures.title: Merchant and Uncle of Juma al Majid
figures.roleDescription: Early Dubai merchant who mentored Juma al Majid
figures.significance: Played a crucial role in launching Juma al Majid's business career by providing him with his first commercial opportunity.
figures.biography:
Ahmad Majid Al Ghurair was Juma al Majid's uncle and an established merchant in Dubai's Deira district. When Juma turned 15 years old, Ahmad asked him to assist at his shop in Deira, providing the young man with his first business experience. Recognizing Juma's potential, Ahmad eventually opened a second small shop for his nephew, which became the foundation for what would later grow into the Juma Al Majid business empire.

Ahmad's mentorship was instrumental in Juma's early commercial development, teaching him the fundamentals of trade and business operations in Dubai's emerging economy. This early guidance and opportunity provided by Ahmad helped establish the business acumen that would later make Juma one of the UAE's most successful entrepreneurs.

figures.name: Mohammad Al Qaz
figures.title: Business Partner and Co-founder
figures.roleDescription: Financier, developer, diplomat, and philanthropist
figures.significance: Co-founded the Mohamed & Juma Al Majid company and served as first Deputy Chairman of the UAE Central Bank.
figures.biography:
Mohamed Abdallah AlGaz was born in Al Dhagaya, Deira in 1920 to a modest family. After his father died when he was young, he was educated at Al Ahmadiya School. During World War II, he worked as a carpenter in Dammam and started a commodity trading operation. He became a trader in textiles and commodities between Middle Eastern markets and entered the historic Switzerland-Dubai-India gold trade in the 1950s after partnering with Juma Al Majid.

AlGaz rose to political prominence as a trusted advisor to Sheikh Rashid bin Saeed al Maktoum and was nominated to the Dubai Municipal Council in 1958. He served as a diplomatic channel between Dubai and Abu Dhabi during land negotiations. After the Indian gold trade declined in the early 1970s, AlGaz and Al Majid invested in franchise rights for international brands including General Electric, Samsung, Pepsi, Nissan, Hyundai, and Kia. After 11 years of partnership, they amicably split their assets, with AlGaz focusing on real estate development.

Post-federation, AlGaz served as the first Deputy Chairman of the Central Bank of the UAE from 1980 into the mid-1990s under Chairman Sheikh Suroor bin Mohammad Al Nahyan. He was instrumental in establishing educational institutions and co-founded the UAE's first charitable society, Beit Al Khair, in 1989 with Juma Al Majid. He died in 2016 at age 96.
achievements: First Deputy Chairman of the Central Bank of the UAE (1980-1990s)
Co-founded Beit Al Khair Society (1989)
Built first three-story building in Dubai's history
UAE Pioneers Award (2018, posthumous)
President's Merit Award (2012)

figures.name: Hana bint Juma Al Majid
figures.title: Mother of Sheikh Mohammed bin Saud Al Qasimi
figures.roleDescription: Member of the Juma Al Majid family through marriage to Al Qasimi ruling family
figures.significance: Connected the Juma Al Majid merchant family to the Al Qasimi ruling family of Ras Al Khaimah through her marriage.
figures.biography:
Hana bint Juma Al Majid is a member of the prominent Juma Al Majid merchant family who married into the Al Qasimi ruling family of Ras Al Khaimah. She became the mother of Sheikh Mohammed bin Saud bin Saqr Al Qasimi, who was born on February 9, 1987, and later became Crown Prince of Ras Al Khaimah.

Her marriage represents an important alliance between one of the UAE's most successful merchant families and the ruling family of Ras Al Khaimah, demonstrating the interconnected nature of prominent Emirati families across the federation.

figures.name: Saleh Abdul Aziz Al Rajhi
figures.title: Sheikh and Founder of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: Founder and eldest brother of the Al Rajhi banking dynasty
figures.significance: Founded Al-Rajhi Bank, the world's largest Islamic banking institution, and established major agricultural projects.
figures.biography:
Sheikh Saleh Abdul Aziz Al Rajhi was born in 1921 in Al-Bukayriyah, Qassim, KSA, as the eldest of the Al Rajhi family. His father, Sheikh Abdul-Aziz Al-Rajhi, was a farmer and trader of lesser means who moved to Riyadh seeking better business prospects. After completing his Quranic studies by age 13, Saleh started working odd jobs in the local marketplace, initially as a porter, then trading scrap items and setting up a bastah (small trading place) in 1937.

In the early 1930s, Al Rajhi began currency exchange business, earning a 5% commission for breaking down Riyals into smaller denominations. By 1947, he opened a formal currency shop in Riyadh and brought his brothers Mohammed and Sulaiman into the business. He sent Sulaiman to Mecca and later Jeddah to expand operations. In 1957, the brothers formally established the beginnings of Al-Rajhi Bank, with Saleh traveling internationally to establish banking relationships and connecting to US banks through his Middle East reputation.

In his later years, Al Rajhi turned from banking to focus on agriculture and real estate. He established the Al Batin Project in 1993, which became the world's largest palm tree project according to Guinness World Records in 2006, and the Durma Project in 1994-1996. He also held stakes in Al Baraka Banking Group and various cement and construction ventures. He died on February 12, 2011, leaving behind a philanthropic legacy and instructions for establishing a foundation to manage continuous charitable endowments.
achievements: Founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957, which became the world's largest Islamic bank
Established Al Batin Project, recognized by Guinness World Records as the world's largest palm tree project
Created Durma Project spanning 760 hectares of specialized palm cultivation
Pioneered currency exchange and money transfer systems in Saudi Arabia

figures.name: Sulaiman Abdul Aziz Al Rajhi
figures.title: Co-founder of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: Brother and co-founder of Al-Rajhi Bank, succeeded Saleh as head of family conglomerate
figures.significance: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank and succeeded his brother Saleh as head of the family business empire.
figures.biography:
Sulaiman Abdul Aziz Al Rajhi was one of the four brothers who founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957, alongside Saleh, Mohamed, and Abdullah. He was brought into the family currency exchange business by his elder brother Saleh, initially hired as a teenager for a salary of $10 to do exchange delivery and messenger jobs for the shop in Riyadh. Sulaiman was later sent by Saleh to Mecca to conduct exchange business for pilgrims and business people, and with his brother's blessing, he remained in Mecca and expanded operations to Jeddah, which was experiencing economic growth at the time.

The brothers conducted their currency business between Riyadh, Mecca, and Jeddah for 25 years, which marked the beginnings of what would become Al-Rajhi Bank. Sulaiman later succeeded his brother Saleh as head of the family conglomerate when Saleh turned his focus to agriculture and real estate in the 1990s. He was involved in various controversies following the September 11 attacks, including allegations related to the SAAR Foundation, though U.S. courts ultimately dismissed all claims against him and the bank in 2014.
achievements: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957
Expanded family currency exchange operations to Mecca and Jeddah
Succeeded as head of Al Rajhi family business conglomerate

figures.name: Mohammed Al Rajhi
figures.title: Co-founder of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: One of the four founding brothers of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.significance: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank alongside his three brothers in 1957.
figures.biography: Mohammed Al Rajhi was one of the four brothers who founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957, working alongside his brothers Saleh, Sulaiman, and Abdullah to establish what would become the world's largest Islamic banking institution. The bank initially began as a group of banking and commercial operations which, in 1978, joined together under the umbrella of the Alrajhi Trading and Exchange Company. The company changed to a joint stock company in 1987, and after two years was rebranded as the Alrajhi Banking and Investment Corporation, before becoming Alrajhi Bank in 2006.
achievements: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957

figures.name: Abdullah Al Rajhi
figures.title: Co-founder of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: One of the four founding brothers of Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.significance: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank alongside his three brothers in 1957.
figures.biography: Abdullah Al Rajhi was one of the four brothers who founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957, working alongside his brothers Saleh, Sulaiman, and Mohammed to establish what would become the world's largest Islamic banking institution. The bank initially began as a group of banking and commercial operations which, in 1978, joined together under the umbrella of the Alrajhi Trading and Exchange Company. The company changed to a joint stock company in 1987, and after two years was rebranded as the Alrajhi Banking and Investment Corporation, before becoming Alrajhi Bank in 2006.
achievements: Co-founded Al-Rajhi Bank in 1957

figures.name: Abdullah bin Sulaiman Alrajhi
figures.title: Chairman of the Board of Directors, Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: Current Chairman of Al-Rajhi Bank's Board of Directors
figures.significance: Serves as Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Rajhi Bank and represents the family's continued leadership of the institution.
figures.biography: Abdullah bin Sulaiman Alrajhi currently serves as the Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Rajhi Bank, one of the four Alrajhi family members on the eleven-member board. He has been involved in defending the bank against various allegations following the September 11 attacks, with U.S. courts ultimately dismissing all claims against him and the bank in 2014. The U.S. Supreme Court issued an order on June 30, 2014, leaving intact the dismissal with prejudice of all claims against the bank and its officers, including Abdullah bin Suleiman Alrajhi, related to the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001.
achievements: Chairman of the Board of Directors at Al-Rajhi Bank
Successfully defended bank against post-9/11 legal challenges

figures.name: Mohammed bin Abdullah Alrajhi
figures.title: Board Director, Al-Rajhi Bank
figures.roleDescription: Board member representing Al-Rajhi family interests
figures.significance: Serves as one of the four Al-Rajhi family members on the bank's Board of Directors.
figures.biography: Mohammed bin Abdullah Alrajhi serves as one of the directors on Al-Rajhi Bank's eleven-member Board of Directors, representing the Al-Rajhi family's continued involvement in the leadership of the world's largest Islamic bank. He is one of four family members who hold board positions, ensuring the family maintains significant influence over the institution they founded in 1957.
achievements: Board Director at Al-Rajhi Bank
